steps.title: Set up the condition
steps.content:
We need to find which value of x is NOT possible given that mean equals median.

The mean is $$\frac{64 + x}{5}$$
The median is the 3rd value when all five numbers are arranged in order. Testing each choice will determine which one fails this condition.

steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice A (6)
steps.content:
When $x = 6$:
Ordered: 6, 14, 15, 17, 18
Median = 15 (the middle value)
$$\text{Mean} = \frac{64 + 6}{5} = \frac{70}{5} = 14$$
Since $15 \neq 14$, the mean and median are not equal. Choice A does NOT satisfy the condition.

steps.title: Backsolving — testing choices B, C, and D
steps.content:
Choice B ($x = 11$): Ordered 11, 14, 15, 17, 18 → median = 15, mean = $\frac{75}{5} = 15$ ✓

Choice C ($x = 16$): Ordered 14, 15, 16, 17, 18 → median = 16, mean = $\frac{80}{5} = 16$ ✓

Choice D ($x = 21$): Ordered 14, 15, 17, 18, 21 → median = 17, mean = $\frac{85}{5} = 17$ ✓

All three satisfy mean = median. Only choice A fails the required condition. That matches choice A — A is correct.
